cards.prompt: DebugView (read via reflection, a debugger-only property) shows a ParameterExpression name with a $ prefix, so a named parameter num prints $num
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; using System.Reflection; static string DV(Expression e)=>(string)typeof(Expression).GetProperty("DebugView",BindingFlags.Instance|BindingFlags.NonPublic).GetValue(e); var numParam = Expression.Parameter(typeof(int), "num"); Console.WriteLine(DV(numParam));

cards.prompt: DebugView adds the C#-literal suffix to a numeric ConstantExpression; a double 10 prints with the D suffix as 10D
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; using System.Reflection; static string DV(Expression e)=>(string)typeof(Expression).GetProperty("DebugView",BindingFlags.Instance|BindingFlags.NonPublic).GetValue(e); double num=10; ConstantExpression expr = Expression.Constant(num); Console.WriteLine(DV(expr));

cards.prompt: Three DebugView rules on one line: parameter $-prefix ($num), double D-suffix (10D), and checked operator #-prefix (AddChecked shown as 1 #+ 2), so the output is $num | 10D | 1 #+ 2
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; using System.Reflection; static string DV(Expression e)=>(string)typeof(Expression).GetProperty("DebugView",BindingFlags.Instance|BindingFlags.NonPublic).GetValue(e); Console.WriteLine($"{DV(Expression.Parameter(typeof(int),"num"))} | {DV(Expression.Constant(10.0))} | {DV(Expression.AddChecked(Expression.Constant(1),Expression.Constant(2)))}");
